L1.area: Changelog file parsing and input processing layer

L2.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where the XML parser does not enable secure processing features, allowing external entity declarations to be processed. This could allow an attacker to read arbitrary files or perform server-side request forgery through crafted XML input.

L3.area: XML SAX-based changelog parser and its parser factory configuration
L3.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where the SAX parser factory and parser instances used for processing changelog files do not enable secure processing or restrict external schema access. An attacker who can supply crafted XML changelog input could exploit this to read arbitrary files or perform server-side request forgery.

ground_truth.reason: The XMLChangeLogSAXParser creates a SAX parser factory and parser instances without enabling FEATURE_SECURE_PROCESSING or restricting external schema access, allowing XML External Entity attacks through crafted changelog files.
